title: Block Comment with {key:Ctrl-V} + {key:I}
subtitle: Add a comment marker to {n} consecutive lines.
summary: {key:Ctrl-V} to enter *visual-block mode*, {key:j} or count+{key:j} to select rows, {key:I} to insert at left edge, type the comment marker, {key:Esc} — the marker appears on every line.

blocks.text: Vim has no built-in "comment this block" command — but *visual-block mode* + capital-{key:I} gives you a one-trick replacement that works in any language.

sequence.note: Insert at left edge of block

blocks.title: Why This Beats Plugins for Most Cases
blocks.text:
Plugins like vim-commentary make this prettier ({key:g}{key:c}{key:c} comments a line). But the *visual-block mode* approach:

  • Has zero dependencies — works on any Vim, anywhere.
  • Works on any prefix marker — `//`, `#`, `--`, `;`, `// TODO:`, anything.
  • Composes with counts — {key:1}{key:0}{key:Ctrl-V}{key:j} for 11 lines.

The trick relies on the block-insert behavior: text typed after {key:I} from *visual-block mode* is *replayed* across all selected rows after {key:Esc}. See [*Block Insert and Append*](#visual.block-insert-append).
